page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, utilizing simultaneous multithreading with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench image processing scoring in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are also highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong PassMark CPU encryption test result, supported by hardware-level AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. The system achieves top-tier status in the PassMark memory score and small-model LLM text generation. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently register in the average range.

Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage requires network-attached storage configurations. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, image processing, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 16 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, image processing, and cryptographic execution, alongside top-tier memory scores and small-model LLM text generation. Other metrics, including multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, fall within the average tier. This hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-lean workloads. It is highly suitable for software compilation, image processing, web serving, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU. It delivers strong single-core performance and top-tier memory scores for compute-bound workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory allocation for high-throughput, compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier memory benchmark scores
Lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network storage
Best suited for software compilation, image processing, and medium-scale LLM inference
